context_note: Uruguay vs Cape Verde, WC2026 Group Stage. URU 71.18% FTR; CPV 8.71%. Draw 20.41% — no RULE8 (<25%). Not extreme favorite — no RULE7 (71.18% < 86%). CPV 8.71% = underdog range (5-15%) → RULE15 applies to CPV primary forwards. Prior match data: URU 1-1 Saudi (URU 6 fouls, 0 YC, 6 OFFSIDES, 14 corners, 10 SOT, 27 shots, 66.8% poss); CPV 0-0 Spain (CPV 1 FOUL, 1 YC, 3 offsides, 1 corner, 1 SOT, 6 shots, 25.7% poss). Uruguay attacking, high-pressing style; Cape Verde ultra-defensive (1 foul vs Spain = barely touched ball). KEY: CPV 1 SOT total vs Spain = very low attacking output; URU 6 offsides vs Saudi = aggressive forward runs.

derivation_notes.URU_goals: From URU OU0.5 Over=85.88%: -ln(0.1412)=1.9576. Cross-check: URU OU1.5 Over=58.17% matches P(≥2|1.9576)=0.582 ✓
derivation_notes.total_check: P(≥3|2.5186)=0.461 vs market OU25 Over=0.4505 — close ✓
derivation_notes.SOT_URU: From URU OU5.5 Over=55.56%: P(≥6|λ=6.0)=0.554 matches market exactly
derivation_notes.SOT_CPV: From CPV OU2.5 Over=42.31%: P(≥3|λ=2.4)=0.430 ≈ market. NOTE: CPV had only 1 SOT vs Spain; market pricing λ=2.4 is much more bullish. Market may reflect longer-run CPV attacking quality vs Spain-specific suppression.

smarkets_prices.secondary_markets_illiquid_note: corners (all lines), penalty_in_match, cards_winner, cards_handicap, player_SOT, player_G_or_A: all returned NO PRICES ~3-4h before kickoff

rules_applied.NO_RULE8: Draw 20.41% < 25% threshold. Not a high-draw match.
rules_applied.RULE15_CPV_forwards: CPV 8.71% win prob = deep underdog range (5-15%). RULE15 applies to Cape Verde primary forwards (Garry Rodrigues, Julio Tavares). Shade their G or A down from naive model estimates.
rules_applied.Q1_URU_offsides: Uruguay had 6 offsides vs Saudi Arabia — extremely high. They make aggressive forward runs and get caught. Against Cape Verde's organized offside trap, expected 4-6 offsides. P(≥2|λ=5)=0.960. Very HIGH YES.
rules_applied.Q2_BTTS_SOT_2H: P(URU ≥1 SOT 2H)=0.950 (virtually certain). P(CPV ≥1 SOT 2H)=0.699 from market-implied λ_CPV_SOT_2H=1.2. Combined: 0.664. But CPV only had 1 SOT total vs Spain — if λ=1 (Spain data), P=0.395. Compromise estimate reflects uncertainty about CPV's true SOT rate vs Uruguay.
rules_applied.Q3_CPV_more_cards: No market price (illiquid). CPV had 1 YC vs Spain; Uruguay had 0 YC vs Saudi. CPV defending deep may accumulate cards. No Smarkets anchor → estimate from logic: ~0.45.
rules_applied.Q4_penalty: No market price (illiquid). Standard WC match P(pen) ≈ 25-30%. Uruguay had 27 shots vs Saudi with no penalty. Default: ~0.28.
rules_applied.Q5_URU_more_fouls: URU 6 fouls vs Saudi (possession team, few fouls). CPV 1 foul vs Spain (barely had ball with 25.7% possession). Against Uruguay's 65% possession, CPV will have more ball → more opportunities to foul. BUT CPV fundamental style is disciplined (1 foul vs world #1). Genuine uncertainty. P(URU > CPV) ≈ 0.42.
rules_applied.Q6_CPV_2plus_SOT_2H: Market implies λ_CPV_SOT=2.4. λ_CPV_SOT_2H=1.2 → P(≥2 in 2H)=0.337. BUT Spain data: CPV only 1 SOT total. Market may be over-generous about CPV attack. Spain result is strong evidence of CPV's attacking limitations. Conservative estimate 0.28.
rules_applied.Q7_URU_wins: Direct FTR market: Uruguay 71.18%. Trust market.
rules_applied.Q8_CPV_scores_2H: λ_CPV_2H=0.316 → P(≥1 goal in 2H)=0.271. 2H winner market: CPV 11.52% to win 2H. Consistent with low but non-zero CPV scoring probability.
rules_applied.Q9_Nuñez_G_or_A: No player market prices (illiquid ~3-4h before KO). Model: λ_URU=1.958. Núñez is Uruguay's primary striker. BEL-IRN comparison: Lukaku (primary BEL striker, similar λ_team=2.16) offered at 58.82%. Núñez similar profile for Uruguay. Estimate: 0.55 (slightly below Lukaku reference since Uruguay λ slightly lower). RULE15 does NOT apply to Núñez (he's on the dominant team).
rules_applied.Q10_URU_6plus_SOT: Direct market: URU OU5.5 SOT Over=55.56%. Matches Poisson check: P(≥6|λ=6.0)=0.554. Trust market. Uruguay had 10 SOT vs Saudi (but that was vs a 33% Saudi team); Cape Verde defense much tougher (only 1 SOT allowed vs Spain). Market already accounts for Cape Verde's defensive strength.

Q1_URU_2plus_offsides.note: Uruguay had 6 offsides vs Saudi in their first match — aggressive forward runs. Against Cape Verde's organized deep block, expected 4-6 offsides. P(≥2|λ=5)=0.960. Even at λ=4: P=0.908. Very high YES. Crowd may underestimate this since 6 offsides is unusual. Edge on YES.

Q2_both_1plus_SOT_2H.note: P(URU ≥1 SOT 2H)≈0.950 (near certain). P(CPV ≥1 SOT 2H): market λ_CPV_SOT_2H=1.2 gives 0.699; Spain raw data gives 0.394. Compromise ~0.65. Combined: 0.950×0.65=0.618. Main uncertainty is CPV attack. Crowd likely 0.55-0.65.

Q3_CPV_more_cards.note: No market price. CPV 1 YC vs Spain, URU 0 YC vs Saudi. CPV defending deep = more card risk. But URU very disciplined (6 fouls only). P(CPV > URU) ≈ 0.45. Coin-flip with slight lean CPV. No strong edge.

Q4_penalty_awarded.note: No market price. Standard WC match: 25-30% penalty probability. Uruguay creating many chances in box (27 shots vs Saudi, no penalty). Default 0.28.

Q5_URU_more_fouls.note: URU 6 fouls vs Saudi (possession style). CPV 1 foul vs Spain (barely had ball). Against Uruguay's 65% possession, CPV expected to touch ball more → more fouls. But CPV is fundamentally disciplined. P(URU > CPV) ≈ 0.42. Slight lean: Cape Verde fouls more defending deep. No strong edge.

Q6_CPV_2plus_SOT_2H.note: Market implies λ_CPV_SOT=2.4 → λ_2H=1.2 → P(≥2 in 2H)=0.337. BUT Spain prior: CPV only 1 SOT in 90 mins (against world #1 in 74% possession game). Compromise estimate 0.28 (discounting market given strong Spain evidence). Crowd likely 0.30-0.40. Lean NO.

Q7_URU_wins.note: Direct FTR market Uruguay=71.18%. Trust market.

Q8_CPV_scores_2H.note: λ_CPV_2H=0.316 → P(≥1 goal in 2H)=0.271. CPV OU0.5 goals 42.94% full match; fraction falling in 2H is ~31-32%. 2H winner market (CPV 11.52%) sets floor. Consistent estimate: 0.27.

Q9_Nuñez_G_or_A.note: No player market price available (illiquid). Model: λ_URU=1.958, Núñez ~45-50% of G+A. BEL comparison: Lukaku (similar λ_team=2.16) offer=58.82%. Núñez slightly lower given URU's lower team λ. Estimate 0.55. RULE15 does NOT apply to Núñez (attacking team). No strong edge direction without market anchor.

Q10_URU_6plus_SOT.note: Direct market: URU OU5.5 SOT Over=55.56% (liquid). Poisson check: P(≥6|λ=6.0)=0.554 ✓. Trust market. URU had 10 SOT vs Saudi but market prices them at λ=6 vs CPV's better defense.

vs_crowd_gaps.Q1: +0.40 (us 0.92 vs crowd 0.52) — MASSIVE lean YES from 6-offside prior
vs_crowd_gaps.Q9: +0.17 (us 0.55 vs crowd 0.38) — big lean YES on Nuñez

submission_note: NO SUBMISSION MADE — match was missed. Crowd estimates recorded for calibration only. Key divergences: Q1 (us 0.92 vs crowd 0.52, +40pp) and Q9 (us 0.55 vs crowd 0.38, +17pp) were our biggest divergences. Post-match results to be filled for learning purposes.

post_match_results.submission_status: NO SUBMISSION — match missed. RBP=0. Results recorded for calibration only.

match_facts.note: Major upset. Uruguay were 71% FTR favorites but drew 2-2 with Cape Verde.

Q1_URU_2plus_offsides.notes: URU 2+ offsides YES. Our 0.92 would have been huge +RBP. Biggest missed gain.

Q2_both_1plus_SOT_2H.notes: Both 1+ SOT 2H: NO. Both on wrong YES side.

Q3_CPV_more_cards.notes: CPV more cards: NO. Our 45% vs crowd 49% — slight edge for us.

Q4_penalty.notes: No penalty: NO. Both correct direction.

Q5_URU_more_fouls.notes: URU more fouls: YES. Both on wrong NO side.

Q6_CPV_2plus_SOT_2H.notes: CPV 2+ SOT 2H: YES. Both severely underestimated CPV.

Q7_URU_wins.notes: URU wins: NO (2-2 draw). Our 0.72 even more wrong than crowd 0.69. MAJOR MISS.

Q8_CPV_scores_2H.notes: CPV scores 2H: YES. Both badly underestimated CPV.

Q9_Nunez_G_or_A.notes: Nunez 0 G+A: NO. Our 0.55 was badly wrong; crowd 0.38 much closer.

Q10_URU_6plus_SOT.notes: URU 6+ SOT: NO. Both slightly wrong on YES side.

post_match_results.hypothetical_summary: Q1 (offside 0.92 vs outcome YES) would have been our best single question ever. But Q7 (URU wins 0.72 → drew 2-2), Q9 (Nunez 0.55 → 0 G+A), Q6 (CPV SOT 0.28 → YES), Q8 (CPV scores 0.27 → YES) would all have been heavy losses. Missing this match was likely net fortunate given the 2-2 upset scenario.
